appDesc.message: All your AI usage limits in one place — Claude, ChatGPT, Grok, Perplexity. Local-only, zero telemetry, minimal permissions.

addPlatformHint.message: TokenYou only gets access to the platforms you enable. You can revoke each one at any time.

loggedOut.message: Sign in to $PLATFORM$ in this browser to see your usage.

fetchError.message: Couldn't read usage. The platform may have changed its API.

pageScriptNote.message: This platform has no usage endpoint, so enabling it adds a small script on its page that counts your sends. It emits an empty signal and never reads your chats.

historyEmpty.message: Not enough history yet — it builds up as you use the platforms you enabled.

notifReset.message: $PLATFORM$: your limit reset — you have quota again.

notifIntro.message: Local alerts while Chrome is open — no servers involved. Grant the permission to enable them.

optIntro.message: For advanced users: define extra services (Cursor, internal dashboards, anything with a JSON usage endpoint) as a JSON array. Each service needs an origin, a usage URL on that same host, and one or more meters mapped with dot-paths into the response.

optSecurityNote.message: Same security model as everything else: a custom service only works after you grant its specific site from the popup, TokenYou only calls the URL you define (https, same host), and nothing ever leaves your browser. The Test button requests the permission and shows exactly what was read.

optSaved.message: Saved. Enable the service from the TokenYou popup.

optNotArray.message: The editor must contain a JSON array of services.

optAuthFail.message: endpoint requires a session — sign in to the site first

optParseFail.message: response received but no meter paths matched — check the dot-paths

emptyBody.message: Enable a platform below. TokenYou will only be able to read that site's usage numbers — never your conversations.
